questions.prompt: In clocked SystemVerilog, why are nonblocking assignments normally used for registers?

choices.A: They make simulation run without a clock
choices.B: Right-hand sides observe the old cycle state and updates commit together
choices.C: They automatically synthesize a faster circuit

questions.explanation: Nonblocking assignment models simultaneous register updates: calculations use pre-edge state and visible updates commit after the event. It does not remove clocks or electrical timing constraints.

questions.prompt: A counter toggles an LED every N input clocks. Ignoring reset, what is the full LED waveform period?
questions.explanation: One toggle changes only half of the square-wave cycle. It takes two groups of N clocks to return the LED to its original level.

questions.prompt: What is the standard bit order of an 8N1 UART frame on the wire?

choices.A: Stop, eight data bits MSB-first, start
choices.B: Start, eight data bits LSB-first, stop
choices.C: Start, parity, eight data bits MSB-first
choices.D: Eight data bits LSB-first with no framing bits

questions.explanation: 8N1 means one start bit, eight data bits sent least significant first, no parity, and one stop bit.

questions.prompt: Which MMIO register behavior most needs an explicit side-effect contract?

choices.B: A status bit that clears when its data register is read
choices.C: A reserved register that always reads zero

questions.explanation: Read-to-clear couples observation with state mutation and can race a new event. Software and hardware need exact same-cycle and acknowledgment semantics.

questions.prompt: When expected and actual waveforms differ, which cycle is usually the most useful debugging anchor?

choices.A: The final cycle where output is visibly wrong
choices.B: The first cycle where any architectural or interface state diverges
choices.C: A random cycle near the middle

questions.explanation: Later mismatches are often consequences. The first divergence is closest to the violated transition or input assumption and sharply narrows the search.
